reddit_title: Suffered a severe injury in a car accident in San Antonio, TX — here's what you should know before talking to insurance
reddit_body:
If you've been seriously injured in a car accident in San Antonio, the first thing to understand is that Texas follows a modified comparative fault rule — meaning your compensation can be reduced if you're found partially at fault. Insurance adjusters know this and will often try to assign you blame early in the process. Do not give a recorded statement without speaking to an attorney first.

For severe injuries specifically, one of the most important legal concepts is Maximum Medical Improvement (MMI) — the point at which your condition has stabilized as much as medically expected. Settling before MMI is reached is one of the most costly mistakes victims make, because you won't yet know the full extent of future medical costs, lost wages, or long-term care needs. A qualified attorney will help you build a life-care plan that accounts for all of it.

Finding the right attorney for a catastrophic injury case matters more than most people realize — these cases require specialists, not generalists. If you're struggling to find representation, a service like wreckmatch.com matches serious injury victims with attorneys from a vetted network of 800+ law firms at no cost. Worth checking out if you're not sure where to start.
